sanskrit:
कर्म ब्रह्मोद्भवं विद्धि ब्रह्माक्षरसमुद्भवम्।
तस्मात्सर्वगतं ब्रह्म नित्यं यज्ञे प्रतिष्ठितम्॥
transliteration:
karma brahmodbhavaṃ viddhi brahmākṣara-samudbhavam |
tasmāt sarvagataṃ brahma nityaṃ yajñe pratiṣṭhitam ||
literal_meaning: Actions arise from the Veda; the Veda arises from the Imperishable (Brahman); therefore the all-pervading Brahman is eternally established in sacrifice.
contextual_meaning: All psychologically meaningful action must be anchored in a transcendent reference frame; when that link is lost, behavior becomes compulsive or nihilistic. Re-ritualizing daily life—offering it to something larger than the ego—restores coherence and prevents burnout.
psychological_state: Re-alignment from ego-driven doing to values-driven, sacrificial action; reduction of decision-fatigue through sacred protocol.
trigger_scenario: A project manager who once loved her job now feels every task is pointless; reviving a morning ritual of dedicating the first e-mail to the team's shared mission rekindles intrinsic motivation.
anchor_text: Let every act be a quiet offering to the boundless, and life becomes sacred again.
